title: CSS Technique  Combat Side Stroke (CSS) is a unique blend of freestyl...
excerpt: CSS Technique  Combat Side Stroke (CSS) is a unique blend of freestyle and breaststroke. It’s quiet and powerful. Having the proper technique and unde...
description: CSS Technique  Combat Side Stroke (CSS) is a unique blend of freestyle and breaststroke. It’s quiet and powerful. Having the proper technique and understanding how the stroke works is key to swimming fast. The way I think about it is in a sequence - a cadence of: “Big arm, little arm, together, shoot.”  COMBAT SIDE STROKE ARM PULL You can swim CSS on either side (right or left) and I like to teach everyone to be able to do so. It’s nice to be able to switch sides during a long ocean swim. In Phase 3 of BUDS, you have a 5.5 mile swim. That’s roughly 8,800 meters. In a pool, if you did 88x100’s on 2 minutes, it would take you about 3 hours. If you swim on your left side, your left arm will be closer to the bottom of the pool. This is called your bottom arm or little arm. Your right arm is the top arm, also known as your big arm.  Bottom Arm = Little Arm Top Arm = Big Arm BOTTOM ARM/LITTLE ARM The bottom arm, I call the little arm, because for 99% of guys, they go faster with a shorter, smaller bottom arm. This is the biggest misconception when it comes to teaching combat side stroke - the bottom arm does not have to go all the way down. It can be just a little circular arm motion - a scull of sorts. When you pull your bottom arm all the way down to your side, you have both of your arms at your side, and your velocity plummets to nearly nothing. Guys that swim like this are easy to spot because they are so herky-jerky. If you hooked them up to a velocity meter, you would see a big spike up and a big spike down. When you shorten the bottom arm, you maintain speed better.  You should play with the length of your bottom arm. If you want to lengthen your stroke, make the bottom arm circle bigger. If you want to speed the stroke up, shorten the bottom arm. It’s nice to have a longer stroke when you are doing 2 mile ocean swims every week in BUDS. But if you are trying to post a really fast 450 for your PST test scores, then a shorter arm is almost always faster.  I think of the bottom arm as a scoop. One huge mistake I see all the time is guys using a straight arm. Don’t do that. Straight arms are weak arms. You need an angle - a power angle. The bottom arm is a circular scoop. Make the circle bigger if you want to make your stroke longer. TOP ARM/BIG ARM The top arm, I call the big arm, because it’s always a normal freestyle pull from top to bottom - it really can’t or shouldn’t be shortened. There are plenty of mistakes that can still be made, though.  The biggest mistake I see is guys doing is not fully rotating over on to their stomach and really reaching out with their arm to lengthen the stroke and put the arm in a position to properly catch the water. I’ve seen a lot of guys with their big arm’s elbow coming out of the water during their pull - which is not good. Another popular mistake is pulling way outside their body or not pulling deep enough - both are something that can be fixed by rotating a little more, too. This can be helped with a slight flutter kick or two after your scissor kick. We will talk more about this later. It is critical to get your fingers pointed towards the bottom of the pool, while keeping your elbow as high on the surface as possible, when you begin your top arm pull. This is called your catch - this is you putting your paddle in the proper position before pulling. Your fingers shouldn’t be glued together or fully spread out - just a little spread in between your fingers. During the recovery phase of the stroke, unlike freestyle, your arm won’t come out of the water. If it does, you’ll get yelled at. This typically happens as practice goes on, guys get tired and lazy and the arms begin to come out of the water to get away from the resistance. Keep it as close to the surface as possible while not having it come out. Long Dog Paddle drill is helpful in practicing this. #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Breaststroke + Side Stroke is fine. I’ve kinda seen it all. Just saw ...
excerpt: Breaststroke + Side Stroke is fine. I’ve kinda seen it all. Just saw a sub 8:30 LCM with breaststroke/dolphin kick. Massive dude. But you want to deve...
description: Breaststroke + Side Stroke is fine. I’ve kinda seen it all. Just saw a sub 8:30 LCM with breaststroke/dolphin kick. Massive dude. But you want to develop a scissors kick if you need to buddy tow. When you have a breaststroke kicker trying to tow, they can’t open up their hip so the guy naturally turns his hip and tows you on their butt. And then the person slides off your butt and you look like two humans side by side  instead of one.  Send me you swimming CSS and I’ll give you some tips.  #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: This guy has an excellent scissors kick. He just needs to connect his...
excerpt: This guy has an excellent scissors kick. He just needs to connect his arm pull with a flutter kick while he’s rotating back over. Snap the hips, ancho...
description: This guy has an excellent scissors kick. He just needs to connect his arm pull with a flutter kick while he’s rotating back over. Snap the hips, anchor that freestyle catch.  Clean up the unnecessary head movements. Keep one eye in the water - that’s as high as you need to breathe.  #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: A really good scissor kick is arguably the most important thing when ...
excerpt: A really good scissor kick is arguably the most important thing when it comes to swimming combat side stroke fast. It’s easy as a coach to see who has...
description: A really good scissor kick is arguably the most important thing when it comes to swimming combat side stroke fast. It’s easy as a coach to see who has potential to be very fast - there’s no greater signal than a fantastic bottom leg of your scissor kick. Making the other changes (rotating, shortening the bottom arm, and getting rid of the giant glide) are easy to make.  #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Replying to @scoosie the bottom arm is a short scoop. It’s like a fre...
excerpt: Replying to @scoosie the bottom arm is a short scoop. It’s like a freestyle catch with a nice power angle. A modified scull. It’s the “get the hell ou...
description: Replying to @scoosie the bottom arm is a short scoop. It’s like a freestyle catch with a nice power angle. A modified scull. It’s the “get the hell out of the way” arm.   I like to see the palm come up as it goes forward.  “Start and stop in streamline” is also important. The only time that arm pauses is when it’s out in front.

title: If you don’t rotate, your paddle is probably out of position to prope...
excerpt: If you don’t rotate, your paddle is probably out of position to properly pull. How’d you like that alliteration? This guy has an excellent scissor kic...
description: If you don’t rotate, your paddle is probably out of position to properly pull. How’d you like that alliteration? This guy has an excellent scissor kick on both sides. All he needs to do is rotate to bring out the best in his BIG DAWG arm. And connect that anchor with a flutter kick. #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: This is an aerobic CSS set that I like to do often.  The focus should...
excerpt: This is an aerobic CSS set that I like to do often.  The focus should be on great technique throughout the set. As the intervals drop, your power need...
description: This is an aerobic CSS set that I like to do often.  The focus should be on great technique throughout the set. As the intervals drop, your power needs to go up. Your flutter kicks should increase. No pullouts off the wall. 1 dolphin kick right into your top arm.  The last 2x50’s are at 9:00 minute pace.  If you can’t make the intervals, change them.  #combatsidestroke

title: You don’t need a lot of flutter kicks in your CSS. But you should hav...
excerpt: You don’t need a lot of flutter kicks in your CSS. But you should have at least 1 that helps anchor your catch and snap your hips.  This clip is also ...
description: You don’t need a lot of flutter kicks in your CSS. But you should have at least 1 that helps anchor your catch and snap your hips.  This clip is also a masterclass on a high elbow catch. Ledecky can get her arm into a nearly 90 degree angle with her fingertips pointed to the bottom underneath her elbow. Strong power angles.  #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Big scissor kicks = a lot of drag.  Sometimes this is needed when bud...
excerpt: Big scissor kicks = a lot of drag.  Sometimes this is needed when buddy towing but for normal CSS, we don’t want the knee to come to your chest like t...
description: Big scissor kicks = a lot of drag.  Sometimes this is needed when buddy towing but for normal CSS, we don’t want the knee to come to your chest like this. On the bottom leg, we need to increase the mobility to create a bigger kick that can whip. Cut the bottom arm in half. Get over top of the barrel with your big dawg arm. And stop gliding so much.  #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Snap those hips as you scissor kick. Use a flutter kick to snap them ...
excerpt: Snap those hips as you scissor kick. Use a flutter kick to snap them back. Combat side stroke isn’t swim solely on your side. #swimming #combatsidestr...
description: Snap those hips as you scissor kick. Use a flutter kick to snap them back. Combat side stroke isn’t swim solely on your side. #swimming #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: CSS Review This guy is a breaststroke kicker. His bottom arm goes all...
excerpt: CSS Review This guy is a breaststroke kicker. His bottom arm goes all the way to hip, leaving him dead in the water with both hands at his side.  Folk...
description: CSS Review This guy is a breaststroke kicker. His bottom arm goes all the way to hip, leaving him dead in the water with both hands at his side.  Folks that kick breaststroke in CSS are forced to rotate because if they didn’t, one leg would come out of the water. Same reason why you can’t do a buddy tow with a breaststroke kick. So moral of the story is - learn how to scissor kick. Now go back and check out that Katie Ledecky video I just made and check out her high elbow vs. this guy. And of course how she is using 1 flutter kick to anchor her pull and snap her hips. #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: YOU MUST ROTATE!! #combatsidestroke

description: YOU MUST ROTATE!! #combatsidestroke

title: This guy has a tiny big dawg arm. You’ll want to rotate your big dawg...
excerpt: This guy has a tiny big dawg arm. You’ll want to rotate your big dawg arm out as far as you can reach and than use a flutter kick to drive over the ba...
description: This guy has a tiny big dawg arm. You’ll want to rotate your big dawg arm out as far as you can reach and than use a flutter kick to drive over the barrel to really get a great arm pull. His bottom arm needs to come in like a freestyle catch, scoop, palm up. Fix your head position. Fix your streamline. Those are very simple to change. #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Don’t stop on the wall - bring your speed off of it. Touch, push off ...
excerpt: Don’t stop on the wall - bring your speed off of it. Touch, push off on your side, rotate onto your stomach, one dolphin kick into your top arm and ge...
description: Don’t stop on the wall - bring your speed off of it. Touch, push off on your side, rotate onto your stomach, one dolphin kick into your top arm and get moving. #swimming #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Breaststroke kicking in your combat sidestroke is fine. Just don’t do...
excerpt: Breaststroke kicking in your combat sidestroke is fine. Just don’t do it for buddy tows. Scissor is far superior when towing. #swimming #combatsidestr...
description: Breaststroke kicking in your combat sidestroke is fine. Just don’t do it for buddy tows. Scissor is far superior when towing. #swimming #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: Sometimes we see guys kicking air like this dude. Sometimes you see a...
excerpt: Sometimes we see guys kicking air like this dude. Sometimes you see a guys bottom arm do the same thing - breaks the surface of the water and when you...
description: Sometimes we see guys kicking air like this dude. Sometimes you see a guys bottom arm do the same thing - breaks the surface of the water and when you pull back you’re pulling air. If we could pull air we could fly. This guy also needs to stop gliding, roatate his hips, and add a flutter kick. Do that and you will go way faster. #swimming #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: I cringe every time I see a dude with straight arms out to his side. ...
excerpt: I cringe every time I see a dude with straight arms out to his side. This is how to do breaststroke pullouts (even tho u should prolly just skip them)...
description: I cringe every time I see a dude with straight arms out to his side. This is how to do breaststroke pullouts (even tho u should prolly just skip them)… From the streamline position, your arms go out, you turn the corner (hands turn back in), and you bring you pull down. This is similar to throwing a med ball down or doing a muscle up.  I like to see the hands nearly touch underneath with big power angles.  Hope @Stefano Razeto #teamrazzo  doesn’t mind us using 3 seconds of him. Check him out he’s got great swimming content.  #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

title: This is a perfect example why gliding during CSS is so counter produc...
excerpt: This is a perfect example why gliding during CSS is so counter productive. He’s going way down under the water, destroying his velocity, and then he h...
description: This is a perfect example why gliding during CSS is so counter productive. He’s going way down under the water, destroying his velocity, and then he has to come back up through the water, again creating more drag.  Same goes for pushing off walls - just push off and continue that speed into your stroke. Don’t hold a glide until your dead in the water. #swimming #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining

description: Bottom arm is sometimes called the “get the hell out of the way” arm.  The top arm in combat sidestroke is your moneymaker.  Make your bottom arm a small scoop.  #swimming #combatsidestroke #navysealtraining
